stdout:
I’ll audit the latest branch state against the issue criteria and prior failed feedback, then verify test/lint evidence before issuing a strict pass/fail verdict.

I’ve confirmed the new proof artifacts; next I’m validating current quality gates directly (`fmt`, `clippy`, tests) and checking whether the prior contract-debt concern is truly resolved.

## Success criteria from issue description

Phase 1 is met: extraction script uses `--no-default-features` and `gf2m` selective opacity (`scripts/verify-lean.sh:24-43`), with blockers/workarounds documented (`proofs/WORKAROUNDS.md`).

Phase 2 is met: `mul_raw` proof exists without `sorry/axiom` (`proofs/Gf2Core/Proofs/Gf2mMulRaw.lean`).

Phase 3 is met in spec-equivalence form: addition and inverse proofs are present and imported (`Gf2mAddition.lean`, `Gf2mInverse.lean`, `proofs/Gf2Core.lean:17-18`).

## Separation of concerns

Pass. `gf2-core` has no dependency on `gf2-coding` (`crates/gf2-core/Cargo.toml`), while `gf2-coding` depends on `gf2-core` (`crates/gf2-coding/Cargo.toml:13`).

## Functional paradigm and performance

Pass. GF(2) arithmetic uses bitwise XOR/shift schoolbook operations (`crates/gf2-core/src/gf2m/mul_raw.rs:41-56`), appropriate for performance-critical code.

## Correctness

Fail. The latest commit changes only docs, asserting overflow panics are “runtime guards” (`mul_raw.rs` docs; commit `93026ec`), but there is still no explicit runtime validation for `m` in `gf2m_mul_raw` (`mul_raw.rs:32-57`). This leaves contract ambiguity/technical debt for invalid `m` handling.

## Testing

Boundary tests include `m=1,63,64,65` and exhaustive GF(16) checks (`mul_raw.rs` tests).  
However, I could not run `cargo fmt/clippy/test` in this environment (execution permission denied), and there is no new in-scope evidence of full Rust lint/test pass for HEAD; only `lake-build` is recorded as passed for `93026ec` (`.jit/gate-runs/ad8e.../result.json`).

## Unsafe isolation

Pass. `gf2-core` still has `#![deny(unsafe_code)]` (`src/lib.rs:35`), and no new unsafe blocks are introduced.

## Documentation

Mixed. Public API docs are comprehensive, but the new panic text claims enforcement not implemented in code (doc/behavior mismatch in `mul_raw`).

## Prior review feedback and dependencies

`issue.dependencies` is empty.  
Most recent feedback on missing inverse proof substance is resolved.  
Most recent feedback on `m`-contract debt remains unresolved in implementation.

VERDICT: FAIL
---
Gate result: FAILED
